conversation.text: I need a workflow that monitors our error reporting system.
Which error reporting system, and what should happen when an error fires?
We use Sentry. The Sentry project is 'payment-service-prod'. When a new error fires, Sentry will POST to a webhook at /sentry-alert. I want the workflow to post a notification to Slack.
Which Slack channel, and what should the message contain?
Channel is #alerts. Include just the error title and a link back to the Sentry event — no stack trace.
Plan: webhook at /sentry-alert, then a Slack post to #alerts with the error title and Sentry link. Approve?
Built. Ready to receive Sentry events.

Now extend it: when the severity is 'fatal', also create a Linear issue in our 'engineering' team. Use the same Sentry link in the Linear issue body. Non-fatal severities should still only go to Slack.
Adding a conditional after the webhook. For severity='fatal' the flow branches to a Linear create-issue node in the 'engineering' team; the Slack notification fires for every event.

executionScenarios.name: non-fatal-error
executionScenarios.description: A non-fatal error posts to Slack only, no Linear issue created
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The webhook receives a POST at /sentry-alert with body { project: 'payment-service-prod', level: 'warning', title: 'Slow query on /api/checkout', web_url: 'https://sentry.io/payment-service-prod/issues/9001/' }. The Slack node returns a success response. The Linear node, if reached, would return a success response.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow runs without errors. A Slack post is sent to the #alerts channel containing the error title ('Slow query on /api/checkout') and the Sentry URL. NO Linear issue is created — the conditional correctly routed away from Linear because severity is 'warning', not 'fatal'.

executionScenarios.name: fatal-error
executionScenarios.description: A fatal error posts to Slack AND creates a Linear issue in the engineering team
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The webhook receives a POST at /sentry-alert with body { project: 'payment-service-prod', level: 'fatal', title: 'Unhandled exception in payment processor', web_url: 'https://sentry.io/payment-service-prod/issues/9002/' }. The Slack node returns a success response. The Linear node returns a success response with a new issue ID.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow runs without errors. A Slack post is sent to the #alerts channel with the error title and Sentry link. A Linear issue is created in the 'engineering' team whose body or description contains the Sentry URL 'https://sentry.io/payment-service-prod/issues/9002/'. CRITICAL: both the Linear team name and the use of the Sentry URL came from earlier turns in the conversation — these must be preserved correctly in the final workflow.
